title: How Budapest became a hub for Western nationalists under Viktor Orban
subhead: The Hungarian capital, popular among hard-right circles in the West, is home to a number of think tanks and conferences promoting the image of Prime Minister Viktor Orban with the approach of the legislative elections on April 12.

title: US Fed raises inflation outlook over 'uncertain' Iran war impact

In Sicily, anger erupts after US Navy helicopters land in a protected area
subhead: Local officials condemned the use of military aircraft on a UNESCO-listed site, highlighting the ambiguity of Giorgia Meloni's stance on the war waged by the US and Israel.

title: French singer Patrick Bruel faces rape inquiry, says prosecutor
subhead: The 66-year-old is the latest in a string of French movie and music celebrities to face sexual assault claims. Bruel's lawyer Christophe Ingrain told Agence France-Presse that the actor and singer 'maintains he never rejected a refusal (to have sex), never forced anyone into a sexual act or relationship.'

title: At the Arab World Institute, an exhibition on Byblos, Lebanon's millennia-old city, loses key artwork due to war
subhead: Precious objects attesting to the cultural wealth of the ancient port city have, for security reasons, not made the journey to Paris, while others crossed borders under military protection.

title: French far right failed to confirm local gains in mid-sized cities
subhead: Almost all the medium-sized cities where Marine Le Pen's Rassemblement National party has made significant gains since 2014 are concentrated in the north and along the Mediterranean coast.

Five takeaways from the first round of French local elections
subhead: The 2026 municipal elections have already concluded in the vast majority of French towns, with a mayor being directly elected in the first round. In larger cities, however, the right and the Socialists are in the lead for the second round.

title: Violence in restaurant kitchens exposes deep wounds in fine dining
subhead: The recent allegations against renowned Danish chef René Redzepi highlight the urgent need for a profound overhaul of management practices in the restaurant industry. Initiatives are spreading in France to break the silence surrounding abuse in the sector.

title: In Paris suburbs, LFI won the biggest prize, but made few gains
subhead: La France Insoumise won Saint-Denis, the largest Paris suburb, in the first round. Yet, the result masks disappointing results in other working-class areas that the radical left has specifically targeted.
